info.name: iFrame Widget
info.summary: Display external Websites in a Dashboard Widget
info.description:
**🔲 iFrame Widget for Nextcloud Dashboard**
This app adds customizable iFrame widgets to your Nextcloud dashboard, allowing you to embed external websites directly within Nextcloud.
## ✨ Features
- **Embed any website** directly in your Nextcloud dashboard - **Customize appearance** with custom titles and icons - **SimpleIcons integration** using the `si:` prefix for thousands of brand icons - **Colorize icons** with a built-in color picker - **Adjustable height** for perfect layout integration - **Responsive design** with extra-wide display option (2 columns) - **Personal widgets** for individual user customization - **Group-based widgets** for targeted content delivery - **Admin controls** for global and group-specific configurations - **Multiple widget slots** up to 5 public and 5 group widgets - **Iframe security controls** with sandbox and allow attribute customization
## 🛠️ Configuration
The widget supports three types of configurations:
### 🌍 Global/Public Widgets (5 slots) Set up widgets that appear on all users' dashboards.
### 👤 Personal Widgets (1 per user) Allow each user to create their own personalized widgets.
### 👥 Group Widgets (5 slots per group) Create widgets that are only visible to specific user groups.
## 🔒 CSP Configuration
If you encounter Content Security Policy errors, this app now adjusts CSP via the Nextcloud PHP CSP API based on the configured widget URLs. Avoid adding a second CSP header at the web server level, as browsers apply the strictest policy. See the full documentation on GitHub for details.
## 💻 Compatibility - Nextcloud 30+
info.author: Bruno Miglar
